The terrain or the weather radar image on the
Answers: PFD and Engine and Warning displays

Questions.QuestionString: The capacitance of a fuel quantity probe depends on
Answers: the area of the plates and the distance between the plates

Questions.QuestionString: Two fuel characteristics sensors located in each tank are
Where are the engine vibration sensors installation?
Which effects on the indicators if a leakage in the static and the pitot system?
Answers: The Altimeter and vertical speed indication will show the same results
The Airspeed indication decreases. The Altimeter and vertical speed indication will show the same results

Questions.QuestionString: Which effects if a blocked static pressure system during climb?
Answers: The Altimeter will continue to display the same altitude during the climb and the Vertical Speed Indication will stay at 0
The Airspeed indication decreases. The Altimeter will continue to display the same altitude during the climb and theVertical Speed Indication will stay at 0

Questions.QuestionString: FDR keep the stored data during a crash the storage device must be protected against high G −loads of up to
The underwater Locator Beacon and the flight recorder can withstand depths of more than
To have enough stability, aircraft gyros must spin at very high speeds of up to
A gyro stability depends on
Answers: First its mass, which is evenly distributed around the spin axis and secondly the rotational speed
First its mass, which is evenly distributed around the spin axis and secondly the distance between the axis and the center of the mass
First its mass, which is evenly distributed around the spin axis, secondly the rotational speed and third the distance between the axis and the center of the mass.

Questions.QuestionString: An erection system can only influence the gyro axis to a maximum
The attitude for PFD (Primary Flight Display) and electronic ADI (Altitude Director Indicator) come from
Answers: An air data and inertial reference unit

Questions.QuestionString: Do the Check ATT. Messages appear on both PFDs at the same time?
Answers: Message only appears on the EWD

Questions.QuestionString: With compass compensation. The B coefficient is
Answers: the difference between the east and west deviations, divided by 2
the difference between the north / south deviations, divided by 2
a misalignment between compass and the aircraft x axis

What are the Secondary colours for use in CRT displays?.

Which alert level requires immediate attention and corrective action?
Answers: Level C in Boeing aircraft or level 1 in Airbus aircraft
Level A in Boeing aircraft or level 3 in Airbus aircraft
Level C in Boeing aircraft or level 3 in Airbus aircraft

Questions.QuestionString: Which alert level requires attention but the corrective action may be delayed?
Answers: Level A in Boeing aircraft or level 3 in Airbus aircraft
Level C in Boeing aircraft or level 1 in Airbus aircraft
Level B in Boeing aircraft or level 2 in Airbus aircraft

Questions.QuestionString: Which alert level informs the Flight Crew but need no corrective action at all?
Answers: Level A in Boeing aircraft or level 1 in Airbus aircraft
Level C in Boeing aircraft or level 1 in Airbus aircraft
Level C in Boeing aircraft or level 3 in Airbus aircraft

Questions.QuestionString: The central warning displays consist of the upper and lower display units on the centre instrument panel. Their function is
Answers: To display messages from the central warning computers and the system synoptic
To display messages from the central warning computers and display permanently some additional aircraft parameters
To display messages from the central warning computers, the system synoptic and display permanently some additional aircraft parameters

Questions.QuestionString: The signal sources for the main inputs of the basic GPWS are
Answers: Radio altimeter, Air Data Computer, glideslope receiver
cabin rate sensors, mach-meters, A.S.I, altimeter.

Questions.QuestionString: Mode 5 of EGPWS becomes active if
Answers: The aircraft deviates below the glideslope during an approach below a radio altitude of 1000 ft
the aircraft approaches the ground with incorrect gear and flap positions
the aircraft loses altitude during a take−off

Questions.QuestionString: Which sensor usually used for vibration indication?
Answers: The forward sensor, but the monitoring unit is able to switch to the aft sensor when the forward sensor fails.
